name: Croissants (Kh)
recipeIngredient: 1 package Compressed or active dry Yeast
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat milk, oil, sugar, and salt in a saucepan; cool to lukewarm.
2. Dissolve yeast in warm water in a large bowl. Add milk mixture and 1 cup flour; beat until smooth. Stir in enough remaining flour to make a soft dough. Turn dough onto a floured surface; continue to work in flour until dough can be kneaded. Knead until smooth and elastic (about 5 minutes). Shape dough into a ball and place in an oiled bowl; turn the top of dough with oil. Cover; let rise in a warm place until doubled in bulk (about 45 minutes).
3. Punch dough down. Roll out on floured surface to form a rectangle about 1/4 inch thick. Cut butter into slices (just soft enough to spread but not melted). Spread over center one-third section of rectangle. Fold each extending side of butter, pressing together the open edges to seal. Roll out again until rectangle is 3/8 inch thick. Turn dough occasionally, lightly floured surface to prevent sticking. Fold in thirds again to make a squarish rectangle. Roll dough and fold again in the same manner. Wrap dough in waxed paper or foil; chill for 30 minutes.
4. If at any time dough oozes butter and becomes sticky while rolling, chill until butter is more firm. Roll and fold again as directed before. Chill dough another 30 minutes.
5. Roll dough into a rectangle about 1/8 inch thick. Cut into strips 6 inches wide. From each strip, cut triangles measuring about 6x8x6 inches. Roll up each triangle from the 6-inch edge, pinching tip to seal. Shape each roll into a crescent. Place, point down, 1½ inches apart on an ungreased baking sheet. Cover; let rise until doubled in bulk (30 to 45 minutes).
6. Brush each roll with mixture of egg yolk and 1 tablespoon milk. Bake at 425°F for 15 minutes, or until brown. Remove from baking sheet and cool on a wire rack. Serve warm.
